chapterTitle: Chapter 4: Inline Elements, Formatting & Semantic Meaning

MCQs.question: Which of the following is an INLINE element?
What is the key difference between <strong> and <b>?

options.text: <strong> carries semantic importance, <b> is just visual

MCQs.question: Which tag would you use to show a chemical formula like H₂O?

options.text: Highlights text with a yellow background

MCQs.question: Which of the following is CORRECT nesting?
Which tag should be used for copyright fine print at the bottom of a page?
How do block elements stack on a page?
What is the correct way to write E = mc² in HTML?
Which tag shows the full form of an abbreviation when hovered?

ShortQuestions.question: Explain the difference between block-level and inline elements with one example of each.
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer:
**Block elements** start on a new line and take up the full available width. Example: `

This is a paragraph.

` **Inline elements** do NOT start on a new line — they flow within text and only take up as much space as their content. Example: `important` ```html

This is a block containing inline text.

```

ShortQuestions.question: Why should you use <strong> instead of <b> in most cases?
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer: `<strong>` is a **semantic** tag that tells browsers, screen readers, and search engines that the text is **genuinely important**. `<b>` is **non-semantic** — it only makes text visually bold without any meaning. Using `<strong>` improves **accessibility, SEO, and code meaning**.

ShortQuestions.question: Write HTML code that shows: "The price was ~~$99.99~~ and is now $59.99 (limited offer)" using appropriate semantic tags.
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer:
```html

The price was $99.99 and is now $59.99 (limited offer)

``` - `` for the old deleted price - `` for the new important price - `` for the fine-print note

ShortQuestions.question: What is the difference between <em> and <i>? Give one appropriate use case for each.
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer:
`` is **semantic** — it means the text is **stressed/emphasized**. Use case: `

I said today, not tomorrow.

` `` is **non-semantic** — it just makes text italic visually. Use case: `

The French word bonjour means hello.

` (foreign word, no emphasis intended)

ShortQuestions.question: Why is it wrong to put a block element inside an inline element? Give an incorrect and corrected example.
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer:
Block elements define large structural sections; inline elements live within text flow. Putting a block inside an inline breaks the document structure. ❌ WRONG: `

This is wrong

` ✅ CORRECT: `

This is correct

` **Rule:** Inline elements go INSIDE block elements, never the reverse.
